sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Heart valve disease presenting in adults: investigation and management
sources.population: Adults with suspected or established heart valve disease, including adults with a cardiac murmur.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, secondary, and specialist cardiovascular care.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for adult murmur assessment, echocardiography, specialist referral, and valve-disease monitoring; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.2 — murmur assessment and echocardiography
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports murmur nature, family and medical history, symptoms, peripheral signs, abnormal ECG, and clinician-led echocardiography review.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.3–1.1.7 — urgent assessment and referral
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports exertional syncope, severe angina or breathlessness, urgent specialist assessment, echocardiography, and referral after echocardiography.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.2 — monitoring without current intervention
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-arranged review and repeat echocardiographic monitoring for established valve disease.
